topics: Atithi Devo Bhava — Guest is God
Avyaya words — words that never change
subject: Sanskrit

sections.heading: ⚔️ The Ancient Spell: Atithi Devo Bhava
sections.body: In India, there is a very powerful spell from our old wisdom books called the Upanishads — 'Atithi Devo Bhava'. This means 'The guest is like God to us'. Just like we welcome Lakshmi into our home during Diwali with full respect, we should welcome every guest with the same love and care. In our country, not just humans but even animals can become beloved guests!
keyPoints: Atithi Devo Bhava means — treat your guest like God
This teaching comes from the ancient Upanishads
In India, guest service is one of the five great duties (Pancha Mahayajnas)

sections.heading: 🐱 Radhika's Very Special Guests
sections.body: Meet Radhika — a brave and loving grandmother on a great quest to practice this ancient spell every single day! Her guests are not humans. Her very special guests are a cat named Majarjari and her four kittens — Nitvi, Mridvi, Shabal, and Bhima. Radhika knows each kitten by name, gives the cat milk, and cares for all of them day and night. Her mind is always on the roof where they stay!
keyPoints: Radhika's guests are a cat and four kittens — Nitvi, Mridvi, Shabal, Bhima
She gives them milk and remembers each one's name with great love
She watches over them all day and night — that is true guest service!

sections.heading: 🧙 The Wizard's Lesson — Teaching the Kittens
sections.body: Radhika is like a wise wizard who wants to teach the ancient spell even to her little kitten guests! She lovingly says to them — 'Do you know Atithi Devo Bhava? You say it too!' She tells them that when guests arrive, we must serve them with full respect. Her granddaughter also learns — we never know when a guest will come, so we must always be ready to serve them well.
keyPoints: Radhika repeats 'Atithi Devo Bhava' again and again throughout the day
She puts her arms around her granddaughter and explains the meaning with love
The lesson: always be ready to serve guests whenever they arrive

sections.heading: 🔮 Magic Words That Never Change — Avyaya
sections.body: In Sanskrit, there are special power words called Avyaya. These words are like magical shields — they never change their form, no matter what! Whether you use them with a boy, a girl, one thing or many things — they stay exactly the same. Words like 'atra' (here), 'tatra' (there), 'sarvatra' (everywhere), 'anyatra' (somewhere else), 'upari' (above), 'bahih' (outside) are all Avyaya words.
keyPoints: Avyaya words never change — no gender, no number, no case change
Examples: atra (here), tatra (there), sarvatra (everywhere), upari (above), antah (inside)
Think of them like the word 'here' in English — it stays the same always!

sections.heading: 🗺️ The Quest Map — Where Is Everything?
sections.body: This chapter gives us a fun quest activity — finding where things are using Avyaya words! Just like in a treasure hunt, you look at a picture and say where each thing is. Is the cat upari (above) or antah (inside)? Is the student tatra (there) or atra (here)? This is how we practise using these magic words in real sentences.
keyPoints: Use atra, tatra, anyatra, sarvatra to say where something is
Use upari (above), adhah (below), bahih (outside), antah (inside), samipe (near), dure (far)
These direction words help us describe the world around us in Sanskrit!

summary: Chapter 9 takes us on a heroic quest to discover one of India's greatest ancient spells — 'Atithi Devo Bhava', meaning the guest is God. Through the story of wise grandmother Radhika, we see this spell in action as she lovingly cares for a cat and her four kittens as her most special guests, knowing each one by name. Radhika teaches her granddaughter that true service means being always ready to welcome and care for guests. The chapter also unlocks the power of Avyaya words in Sanskrit — magical words like atra, tatra, upari, and bahih that never change their form, no matter how you use them. Together, these lessons teach us both a life value and a grammar skill, making us true wizards of the Sanskrit realm!
